details.text:
- The decision of Allah has already come, so don’t rush to it (or seek to hasten it). ‘Ataa (to come) is in the past tense (Al-Maadi) and Tasta’jilu (to rush) is in the present tense (Al-Mudaari’).
- Here the past tense and present tense have been brought together which is a powerful combination. That is as the Faa’il (doer) in the first part of the Ayah is Allah and the matter revolves around Him while the second part related to not seeking to rush it is about us. Allah is the owner of all the time where there is no difference between the future and the past, whilst we are the ones who are waiting for it in the future. Therefore, when He refers to Himself, he refers to a future matter using the past tense and when He refers to us, He switches to the present tense form.
- This can mean that they commit Shirk because of Shaytan or by making Shaytan a partner.

- Majority view is that this related to abrogation whereby they accuse the Messenger ﷺ of making it up due to changing it. A minority view is that this relates to the differences in verses that appear similar, may begin in the same way or have differences within them which is met by the accusation of making them up.

- Here the descent of the Qur’an is attached to رُوحُ ٱلْقُدُسِ referring to Jibreel (as), however normally, the descent of the Qur’an is attached to Allah (swt). Based on evidence like this, there is a minority view that when Allah states in the Qur’an that “we have sent down the Qur’an” that it is referring to Allah and Jibreel together. However, the descending of the revelation by Jibreel is qualified here by the statement مِن رَّبِّكَ (from your Lord) indicating that Jibreel brings it down from his Lord.
Derivative: يُلْحِدُونَ means a deviation off course like when an arrow deviates from the target.

Derivative: أَعْجَمِىٌّ means foreigner or non-Arab. It also means someone who stutters or has difficulty putting syllables and speech together. It is the counter to عَرب (Arab) which indicates to being well spoken and clear whilst أَعْجَمِ is someone who cannot speak clearly and is a condescending term for non-Arabs.

Derivative: ٱلْغَٰفِلُونَ are those who are completely heedless. After, mentioning two verses prior to this that they were satisfied with disbelief, here it is describing their state to be one which is complete tranquil in their environment and heedless of anything outside of that.
- Second time employed in this Surah and its meaning is that: “There is no doubt it” or also “I swear by Allah”.

- Allah made them taste the لِبَاسَ (garment/clothing) of hunger and fear. Clothing means the hunger and fear wrapped the city like clothing wraps around you.
- Observation that Allah mentions hunger first and fear second. It is observable that hunger proceeds fear, in the case where fear in a place is associated with crime which is proceeded by hunger.

Derivative: Halal (حَلَال) here is not referring to the meat that is slaughtered in a Halal manner but rather refers to money that is not earned by cheating, scamming, gambling and usury i.e., through dishonest and evil means.

Derivative: It means to pronounce someone’s name out loud when you slaughter which was original rooted to هِلال (the new cresent moon) because when they would see it, they would loudly pronounce their false Gods names in the form of a major event.

- It is a figure of speech: Don’t say based upon what comes out of your mouths, literally: ‘what your tongues describe’ or ‘your descriptive tongues’, which is nothing more than a lie – that this is Halal and this is Haram.
details.arabic_part: وَلَا   تَقُولُوا۟   لِمَا   تَصِفُ   أَلْسِنَتُكُمُ   ٱلْكَذِبَ

details.text:
Derivative: هَادُوا۟
Root: ه و د – هادَ – يَهودُ – It means to be or become Jewish or in its literal meaning: to turn back to Allah.
Derivative: To be overcome by emotion. جَهَالَة (Jahaalah) is not only Ghaflah (heedlessness) or the absence of knowledge (or ignorance). It also means someone who gave in to their temptations.

Derivative: يَعْقِلُونَ (to think) takes less effort than يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (to think deeply) which is mentioned in the previous verse. In the case where the night, day, sun, moon and stars are signs mentioned in this verse are more available than the crops mentioned in previous verse.
Derivative: Solely focused. حَنَف means without bias. جَنَف means biased.
Derivative: قَانِت is one of the words for obedience but is characterised by concentration, joy, subservience and humility.

Derivative: أُمَّةً
Root: أُمَّةً (nation) comes from أَ م م – أَمَّ- يَؤُمَّ which means Al-Qasd (القَصْد) which means to have a direction and to aim for something. Therefore, أُمَّةً is a nation that has a singular direction.
Derivative: The Haal (state or condition) of being grateful.
Derivative: To select someone based upon special qualities that person possesses.

Derivative: This plural of نَعْمَة (blessings) differs from the plural نِعَم (blessings). It indicates to the few favours and not all favours that Allah has bestowed. It is impossible to be grateful for

- Here “ما كانَ” (He was not) was employed in the case where “مَا” is used for the purpose of refutation, to refute that he was from the Mushrikeen (idolators) as opposed to verse 120 of this Surah where Allah said: “لَمْ يَكُ” (He was not) using “لَمْ” which was in response to a rhetorical question and not for the purpose of refutation.

- There are three levels of calling to the path of Allah mentioned in the Verse: 1) بِٱلْحِكْمَةِ – With wisdom 2) ٱلْمَوْعِظَةِ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ – Good, beautiful counsel or direction and 3) جَٰدِلْهُم بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ – debate strongly with that which is better. Theoretically these three levels of engagement can be applied to three groups within society: Intellectuals (Wisdom), the masses (good, beautiful counsel or direction) and fierce defenders of the status quo (argue strongly with that which is better).

- وَجَٰدِلْهُم - And debate/argue with them strongly with that which is أَحْسَنُ i.e., with the arguments which are better not the best (ألأحْسَن). You bring a better response to them or if they use foul language you respond back with clean language and better manners.

Derivative: عًاقَبَ – It derives from عَقِبٌ which means heel. أَعْقَبَ means to follow someone closely. إعْقاب – عاقَبَ – يُعَاقِبُ means to punish but relates to a reactionary or retaliatory punishment.

Derivative: In Arabic مَعَ can mean physically being with someone and it can also refer to comradery, brotherhood and agreement. Allah states he is with (مَعَ) those who have Taqwa and are Muhsin, which means that they have Allah’s supports in this mission and that is sufficient for them regardless of what they face.

Derivative: To remember: It is less in terms of thought effort than يَعْقِلُونَ in the previous verse which was less than يَتَفَكَّرُونَ in the verse prior to that. This therefore indicates to the least amount of thought effort. Although the Shaddah in يَذَّكَّرُونَ indicates Mubaalaghah, it is not prolonged and is less than يَتَذَكَّرُونَ.
Derivative: To create and distribute

- This construct is called An-Na’t As-Sababi (النَّعْتُ السَّبَبِي) where the Siffah precedes the Mawsuf and does not follow it in status. It is actually أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ مُخْتَلِفَةٌ (Its colours are different).
Derivative: It can refer to revelation and to the Angel Jibreel (AS). Ruh has multiple applications in respect to the revelation.

Derivative: The plural is used here and not the singular form. The Messenger of Allah came across multiple generations as though Allah is combining those generations and calling them altogether in one shout, telling you all the same thing: Warn (all of you) that there is no one to be worshipped and obeyed except Myself.

Derivative: A significance of being reminded often that your Ilaah is Wahid (One) is that it removes all scapegoats for those in polytheist societies. Reinforcing that all matters return solely to Allah and He cannot be denied or ignored.

- Roughly translated means “no doubt about it”. Ibn Farees also believes that it means: “I swear by Allah” like “Wallahi”. لَا جَرَمَ Always comes with أَنَّ and because of that it acts as extra emphasis as أَنَّ already implies emphasis.

Derivative: It means legends, stories of old times giving the impression that they are just stories, myths or folk tales which no longer have merit or relevancy in the current age. أَسَٰطِيرُ is the plural of أُسْطُورَة. It may have come to Arabic from Greek “Ustouriyah” from which we get the English word “story”.
the mention of carrying load repeated three times in the verse. not only carry your own complete load, but also the load of those you misled. how terrible is the load that you will be carrying!

- It means that firstly they didn’t realise that they are misguiding others and secondly, that they successfully misguided others and those others had no knowledge, and as such they were so easily misguided.
details.arabic_part: وَمِنْ   أَوْزَارِ   ٱلَّذِينَ   يُضِلُّونَهُم   بِغَيْرِ   عِلْمٍ

details.text: - The strongest part of the building is the قَوَاعِد (foundations). When a storm or something similar comes it attacks everything but leaves the foundations. Allah says: That when I attack, I start at the foundation. Then the ceiling comes down upon them.
Derivative: From شاقَّ – to argue, debate, to viciously oppose.

- The response here has a deep meaning indicative of the one who is praising based on having tasted the revelation and have a relationship with it, in the case where خَيْرًا (Good) means emphatically “Good” i.e., Amazing. This is the Ihsan for which the great reward is provided.
- In Jannah they will have what they want. They do not need to request it, just merely want it, and they will have it.

- The evil and harms of what they had done came to attack them. Allah does not say that the punishment of Allah attacked them, but rather that it was your own deeds and sins that attacked you. Then the very thing that they poked fun at encircled and took hold of them.

- Allah’s response to those employing arguments of predestination to justify their disbelief is not to delve into their argument but rather simply affirm that: The responsibility of the Messengers is only to communicate the message clearly. Meaning: Had you been listening to the Qur’an you would not have asked such a dishonest question.
details.arabic_part: فَهَلْ   عَلَى   ٱلرُّسُلِ   إِلَّا   ٱلْبَلَٰغُ   ٱلْمُبِينُ

details.text: Derivative: Meaning the forces of rebellion against Allah.
Derivative: The meaning is that appointed and also that we have raised.
Derivative: To become materialised, become a reality for them and also that it was rightfully deserved for them in the case where they earned it (i.e., the misguidance).
- On the contrary, it is a promise that is mandated upon Him truly, meaning that it is a demand of Allah’s justice that He will bring. It is a promise upon Him, mandated upon Him and is true.
- Meaning that they put a lot of effort into the oaths they swear; that they are really loud and emotional when they make their oaths.

- The assumption is that those who lie are aware but here Allah is saying: So that the disbelievers will know of the Day of Judgment that they had been lying. This perhaps addresses the reality of those who lie for a long time until they believe the lie to be true.
details.arabic_part: وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا كَاذِبِينَ

details.text: Derivative: نُّطْفَةٍ (sperm/fluid) – It literally means something you want to wash off your clothes; something dirty.
- Just yesterday you were a dirty fluid and now all of a sudden you think you can argue against Me. This is very powerful as Allah is putting the human being is his place in this Ayah.
details.arabic_part: خَلَقَ   ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ   مِن   نُّطْفَةٍ   فَإِذَا   هُوَ   خَصِيمٌ   مُّبِينٌ

details.text: Derivative: The ما here is ما المَصْدَرِيَة (Maa Al-Masdariyah) and carries the meaning of أَنْ and the ما المَصْدَرِيَة is more common in classical Arabic than أَنْ المَصْدَرِيَة (An Al-Masdariyah).
- In Arabic you cannot go to a verb after بَعْدَ (after) and you have to place an Adaat ul-Masdar (such as Maa or An) in the middle between them.

- The original context of the Ahlu dh-Dhikr (أَهْلَ ٱلذِّكْر) are the people of the Book and the scholars among them. The term Adh-Dhikr (الذِّكْر) is used in the Qur’an to describe both the Qur’an and the previous books and so the Ahl ul-Kitab (people of the book) are also Ahlu dh-Dhikr.

- Here Allah is stating that He sent down the Dhikr (i.e., the Qur’an) to the Messenger so that he can explain to them what was sent down to them i.e., their previous scripture, where the Qur’an will qualify and clarify their previous scriptures.
details.arabic_part: وَأَنزَلْنَآ   إِلَيْكَ   ٱلذِّكْرَ   لِتُبَيِّنَ   لِلنَّاسِ   مَا   نُزِّلَ   إِلَيْهِمْ

details.text: - Observation: This does not mean that Allah will sink them into the earth but rather sink the earth with them in it.
Derivative: The word داخِر (Daakhir) combines three things within its meaning: Humiliation, embarrassment and powerlessness.

Derivative: يَتَفَيَّؤُا
Root: فاء يَفيءُ (ف ي ء) – The root means to return like رَجَعَ (Raja’a)
- يَتَفَيَّؤُا of the shadows means to withdraw and pull back.

- This is called “Al-Mashghulu ‘Anhu” (المَشْغُولُ عَنْهُ) in Arabic grammar – It is when you have the Maf’ul Bihi (مَفْعول بِهِ) which in this case is Al-An’aam/cattle (ٱلْأَنْعَٰمَ), followed by the verb which is خَلَقَ (He created) and then by the Maf’ul Bihi again with the هَا (Dameer referring back to the An’aam). The Mashghul ‘Anhu is used when the Maf’ul Bihi is something that the speaker takes a lot of pride in or something he really wants you to think about. (This is then followed by the mention of the numerous benefits that can be attained from the cattle).

Derivative: وَاصِبًا (Waasib) means to be the same and to remain unchanged over time– forever and ever, permanently. In relation to the Deen, this means that the essential teachings will remain unchanged and so any change will have come from us. We would have altered it as the original teachings are all the same.

Derivative: تَجْـَٔرُونَ
Root: ج أ ر – جأَرَ – يَجأَر : To cry out in anguish like when an animal is being attacked and makes noises.
Derivative: Meaning that you cry out to Allah in that desperate manner like animals do when they are in a desperate situation.
Derivative: To alleviate and to remove.

لِيَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ آتَيْنَاهُمْ ۚ فَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ

- It begins in the third person لِيَكْفُرُوا (let them show ingratitude) and then switches to the first person فَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ (then enjoy yourselves). And continues in the third person: Then you will come to know and find out the consequences! (فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ). It is threatening language: From third person to more direct second person.

Derivative: Allah is swearing my Himself. This is a progression. In the previous Surah Allah swore by something that He had not sworn by, which was the Prophet ﷺ in relation to the madness of the people of Lut and here Allah is even angrier in relation to the craziness of Quraish.
- By Allah (Allah is swearing by Himself) you will be so deeply interrogated (لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ), I swear about it, concerning the things that you have been making up (تَفْتَرُونَ).

Derivative: كَظِيمٌ
Root: ك ظ م – كَظَمَ – To swallow something big.
- He is deeply depressed and swallowing his sadness.

Derivative: يَتَوَٰرَىٰ
- It means to hide after you’ve been defeated (dealt humiliation).

- And to Allah belongs the highest examples - This means several things: When Allah talks about Himself, he provides the highest forms of example to explain your relationship with him. Here, He does not give examples of Himself but rather makes examples of the relationship He has with us. Also, it means that Allah owns the best possible examples, so when He provides an example, it is the best possible example.

- Why was وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (And they can’t bring it forward) mentioned here and not just لَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ (they cannot delay it)? Because once the questioning or punishment begins, people will be saying let it just end. No, you cannot bring it to an end earlier.

Derivative: It means to be left abandoned – from أَفْرَطَ. أَفْرَطَ شَيْءا means he forgot something and left it behind. Previously the man was thinking about forgetting his (newborn) daughter and leaving her. Allah is saying that he will put these people in the fire and then they will be مُّفْرَط (Mufrat) – be left and forgotten.

- Just like Allah brings the earth to life with water, revelation will bring life to the hearts but for that to happen you have to listen. Just like water needs to be absorbed into the earth, these words have to be absorbed into the heart. That’s why it is a sign for the nation that is willing to listen.
details.arabic_part: إِنَّ   فِى   ذَٰلِكَ   لَءَايَةً   لِّقَوْمٍ   يَسْمَعُونَ

details.text:
Derivative: عِبْرَةً
Root: ع ب ر – عَبَرَ – يَعْبُرُ – It means to crossover something.
Derivative: عِبْرَةً (‘Ibrah) literally means to make the tear cross over the eye. It means a powerful lesson that should move you to tears.

Derivative: سَآئِغًا
Root: س و غ – سَاغَ – It means شَرِبَ – to drink
Derivative: سائغ (Saa’igh) A drink that goes down easily and want more and more of it.
Derivative: A sweet drink and can also refer to wine.

Derivative: Provision – Points to the fact that Allah also takes credit for human creativity. In the case where you take what Allah has provided, make something else from it and then even that is Rizq Hasan رِزْقًا حَسَنًا(a wholesome provision). You took that, then made something else out of it and that also goes to my credit.
- it means a nation that tries to understand. When you have a Siffah (description word) coming in the form of a verb then it means that an attempt is being made.

Derivative: يَعْرِشُونَ
- To build high. ‘Arsh (عَرْش) is something elevated and high. Also, something planted deep into the ground and then raised high like a trellis.

Derivative: يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
Derivative: A people that wants to think deeply. It requires greater effort than يَعْقِلونَ.

Derivative: ٱسْلُكِى
Root: س ل ك – سَلَكَ – To pursue/follow (a path)
Derivative: ذُلُلًا (Dhulal) comes from ذَليل (Dhaleel). It means powerless and also means putting yourself down or being brought down in humility.

the bee acting in accordance with the revelation of allah is a reminder for us to follow the revelation of allah. allah commands it what to eat, where to go and what path to take. similarly, what path are we taking?

- Every path the bee takes is one that He dictated. سُبُلَ رَبِّكِ (The paths of your Lord): The bee is following revelation. The paths it takes are revealed to it. The bee is in complete humility and submission to Allah (ذُلُلًا). The path that it is going to take is one which will be taken humbly.

- Observation: What is easier observe? The milk coming from the cow or the honey coming from the bee? The process is very direct with the milk of the cow but the process through which honey is derived, the paths the bee takes to get to the flower, to extract nectar, return to the hive and then from its belly extract honey is something requiring deep thought. Hence, يَعْقِلُونَ is suitable in respect to the milk extracted from cows and يَتَفَكَّرُونَ for the honey extracted from bees.

- How different its textures are. This is An-Na’t As-Sababi (النَّعْتُ السَّبَبِي) where the Siffah precedes the Mawsuf: مُخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ (Mukhtalfan Alwaanuhu). It is not أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ مُخْتَلِفَةٌ (Its colours/textures are different) which would be a Jumlah Ismiyah.

Derivative: Truly compassionate. Ra’ouf also refers to someone who understands what you are going through. الرَّأْفَةٌ (Ar-Ra’fah) means compassion, mercy and also means someone who understands what you are going through.
Derivative: Means to be turned back.
Derivative: Ugly, less than and beneath everything, weak, pathetic and despicable, similar to a return to childhood.

- The age itself become ugly. You are unable to fend for yourself fully, your skin becomes wrinkly, you disturb others. You reach that despicable age. Some Allah does not take in death when you are in good shape but rather allows you to get old an deteriorate.
- Allah is All-Knowledgeable All-Powerful. Allah’s knowledge and capability does not deteriorate. It is our knowledge and capability that does. Therefore, the contrast is made.

- Transition from previous verse mentioning the curing quality of honey. Death, old age and senility where he loses his memory and no longer knows what he had previously known are all matters for which there is no cure for.
details.arabic_part: يُرَدُّ   إِلَىٰٓ   أَرْذَلِ   ٱلْعُمُرِ   لِكَىْ   لَا   يَعْلَمَ   بَعْدَ   عِلْمٍ   شَيْـًٔا

details.text:
- From a grammatical perspective: The “Baa’” in “بِرَآدِّى” is the Jawaab of the “ما” (maa). It is a “Baa’ Zaa’idah” (added Baa’) in Nahw (grammar).
- بِرَآدِّى رِزْقِهِمْ is Mudaaf and Mudaaf Ilaihi (مُضاف وَمُضاف إلَيْهِ) with the meaning of Maf’ool Bihi (مَفْعول به).
Derivative: Two meanings one of denial and one of ingratitude. The Kufr (كُفْر) of a Nim’ah (blessing/favour) which means ingratitude and the Kufr of Rafd (رَفْض) which means refusal or denial.

the contrast between the ‘abd mamluk who has no power over anything and the one whom allah has bestowed a good rizq (provision) to. the bees, cattle and other creations of allah are ‘abd mamluk, they are completely owned and under allah’s authority. you however have been given choice and been honoured and can spend from your provision in charity out of your free will. how can these two examples in the verse be the same and equal.

- This is ‘Atf Bayaan (عَطْف بَيان) where you bring another noun after the first noun. it is just another way of saying the first noun.
- It states ‘Abd Mamlook which literally means an owned slave which is peculiar as a slave by definition is owned. This is the use of hyperbole and overemphasis which means that he is completely under control, has no free will whatsoever and is in absolute slavery.

after allah presents the example of two men in the case where the first man does not possess the faculty of speech, is unable to do anything, is a burden upon his master and achieves nothing good (or productive) when directed to undertake it, it is expected that the second man would be one who is useful to his master, however, allah undertakes an amazing switch. instead of telling you about “you” useful slave, i will tell you about “my” useful slave: the one who commands justice and follows the straight path. a slave owner does not expect his slave to command justice but allah does.

In addition, the inability of the first man (slave) was his inability to speak in contrast with the second man who commands justice and speaks up. Analogy can be made with the Quraish at that time where instead of speaking up they preferred to remain silent and not speak about injustice, and that person is like a burden on the earth and waste of space bringing no benefit.

- This means to be committed upon a straight path i.e., whatever happens he will stay on course. It follows the commanding of justice as the one who commands justice will make enemies and face pressures to change course. So, Allah mentions right after commanding justice that he stays on course.

Derivative: لَمْحِ
Root: From لَمَحَ (ل م ح) – It means to glance at something very briefly
- Literally going to happen in the blink of the eye.

- This is the second time this absence of knowledge is mentioned in this Surah. The first in relation to the one who is reversed returning to the decrepit and senile age where he knows nothing which is like the newborn mentioned here.

- This is the third time Allah has mentioned Batn (stomach) or Butoon (stomachs) in this Surah. The first in relation to what Allah extracts from the cattle, second from bees and here from mothers.
- This comes after the previous verse which discusses the coming of the Day of judgement. Allah discusses here how He brings you out from your mothers womb and likewise on the Day of judgement Allah will bring out the burdens from the earth.

Derivative: مُسَخَّرَٰتٍ
Root: Comes from Taskheer – Sakh’khara (تَسْخِير - سَخَّرَ). Taskheer means to overpower something, keep it in place and subdue it
Derivative: This can also be Ism ul-Jam’ (اسْمُ الجَمْع) i.e., collective plural with the meaning of birds.
Derivative: It means the intense climate and winds of the sky.

- In the previous verse Allah states that he provides for us hearing, sight and hearts. Here he is skipping the hearing because you are already hearing the message by reading the verse. The focus in this verse is upon observation and to seeing.
Derivative: Wool of sheep and goats.
Derivative: In modern Arabic refers to furniture but in old Arabic it refers to all household items, whether small and large, and even slaves i.e., anything that is utilised in the house.
Derivative: All animal hair including that of humans.
Derivative: The hair of camels and feathers of birds

Derivative: The meaning of Zha’n (ظَعْن) is Safar (سَفَر): Travel. It is a type of travel where you are regularly travelling or you make lots of stops from one place to another and so on, which is descriptive of Bedouin life.

Derivative: Istikhfaaf (اِسْتِخْفاف) means to try and keep something light. It also has another meaning, which is a social meaning and it is to keep a people weak or to keep their influence light which is something Fir’awn attempted: اسْتَخَفَّ قَوْمَهُ – He tried to keep his people lighyt and without influence in the land.
Derivative: A means of utilising and also can mean a means for enjoyment.

Derivative: سَرَٰبِيلَ
- Previously mentioned (in verse 50 of Surah Ibrahim) the Saraabeel which will be part of the punishment of hell. Here it is the Saraabeel of the life of this world which is a means of luxury referring to the whole outfit or attire that is worn which protect you from heat and in war.
Derivative: أَكْنان – Aknaan is the plural of أَكِنَّة or كَنٌّ is to keep something out of harm’s way and here the mountains and caves are a shelter from the harsh desert environment.

Derivative: Here Allah state: Most of them are “the” disbelievers. Why are they not just disbelievers in the Nakirah (indefinite) form which is usual in a Jumlah Ismiyah (noun or nominal sentence)? What is special about this people to provide them with this distinction and are not covered by the regular Khabar (information) in a nominal sentence? The distinction is to indicate that they are the ultimate disbelievers referring to the Quraish. They heard the Quran directly from the mouth of the Prophet ﷺ, in their own language and dealing with their own realities whilst their own survival in the city was impossible without the miraculous favours of Allah coming to them and yet they disbelieve. Hence, they are “the” ultimate disbelievers and not just ‘regular’ disbelievers!

Derivative: يُؤْذَنُ
- one meaning is to be given an ear i.e., to be listened to. the other meaning is to be given permission.

Derivative: It is from إعْتاب (I’taab) – عاتَبَ – يُعاتِبُ – It means to reprimand, scold, to be harsh in advice or tough with somebody for their own benefit. اِسْتَعْتَبَ (Ista’taba) is the opposite: To try and make somebody happy or content with you again and to seek amends with somebody following a reprimand.
- The disbelievers won’t be permitted to speak on the Day of Judgement i.e., to argue, bring excuses or try and make amends.

- Those who were made partners with Allah will dissociate themselves from those who used to make them partners which indicates that they were to unaware of the fact that they had been taken as partners. As such they declare those who made them partners with Allah to be liars. An example is Prophets or righteous people who were taken after their deaths as partners to Allah.
- Literally to throw submission. It means to give yourself up, to throw yourself before somebody. It figuratively means to grovel at someone’s feet.

- Why does Allah increase them in the torture and punishment over and beyond the torture or punishment already there? 1) They disbelieved 2) They stopped themselves and others from the path of Allah – you became an enemy to the mission, cause and truth 3) They caused corruption and were not only corrupt in themselves.
- عَذَابًا is Nakirah (indefinite) and ٱلْعَذَابِ is Ma’rifah (definite)

Derivative: تِبْيَٰنًا (Tibyaan) is the Mubaalaghah (exaggerated, emphasised) form of تَبْيِين (Tabyeen) from بَيَّنَ (Bayyana) which is a rare Mubaalaghah pattern. It means an extremely elaborate clarification (of everything).
Derivative: It means straight and is a Masdar used as an adjective. The adjective is made into a Mudaaf for hyperbole reasons.
- This actually means سَبيلُ القَصْد (Sabeel ul-Qasd i.e., an inversion of order from قَصْدُ ٱلسَّبِيل) and represents a form of Mubaalaghah (exaggeration).
Derivative: Rebellion and normally refers in the Qur’an to be rebellion against Allah.

- Distinction being made between ٱلْفَحْشَآءِ and ٱلْمُنكَر is being made here. ٱلْفَحْشَآءِ is rude, obscene, vulgar behaviour, could include obscene speech, art, businesses, company etc. ٱلْمُنكَر is what is known and recognised to be evil.

- The distinction between ٱلْعَدْلِ (justice) and الإِحْسَٰنِ (excellence). ٱلْعَدْلِ is the lowest you should reach and the highest you should reach is الإِحْسَٰنِ thus the entire spectrum of good deeds is captured between the two.

Derivative: أَنكَٰثًا
Root: It comes from نَكَثَ to tear weaving apart or to undo unravel wool.
Derivative: Instead of نَكَثاً which means to tear or unravel weaving it employs أَنكَٰثًا which indicates weave by weave or part by part.
Derivative: أَرْبَىٰ mean more grown, more powerful or ahead. It is the Ism Tafdeel form of Ribaa (رِبا) which means growth.

- مَن can be the continuous Faa’il (doer) of يَشَآءُ (he wills) in which case we would translate it as He (Allah) misguides whom He wills and guides whom He wills. Or we could say that مَن is the Faa’il (doer) referring to the person with the meaning that Allah misguides whomever wants to be misguided and guides whomever wants to be guided, whereby they are simultaneous – Allah’s will and man’s will.
details.arabic_part: يُضِلُّ   مَن   يَشَآءُ   وَيَهْدِى   مَن   يَشَآءُ

details.text: Derivative: يَنفَدُ – نَفِدَ is the opposite of بَقِيَ which means to remain. It means to run out.

- Allah states: Whatever you have, will run out (يَنفَدُ) whilst whatever Allah has, remains (بَاقٍ). Here يَنفَدُ is a Fi’l (verb) whilst بَاقٍ is an Ism (noun). That is as what remains is permanent like an Ism is (noun) whilst what runs out is like a Fi’l (verb) is, which is temporary.
details.arabic_part: مَا   عِندَكُمْ   يَنفَدُ  ۖ  وَمَا   عِندَ   ٱللَّهِ   بَاقٍ

details.text: - here the righteous act is qualified by belief as a good deed can be undertaken without Iman which is devoid of its fruits.
details.arabic_part: مَنْ   عَمِلَ   صَٰلِحًا   مِّن   ذَكَرٍ   أَوْ   أُنثَىٰ   وَهُوَ   مُؤْمِنٌ

details.text: - صَٰلِحًا could be regarded as being a Maf’ool Bihi (object of the verb) in which case it would mean whoever did a single good or righteous deed. صَٰلِحًا could also be regarded as being a Haal (adverb) in which case it means whoever acted righteously.

Derivative: إِنَّهُ
- Here إِنَّهُ has a Dameer (هُ) that does not appear to return back to anything. It is here as a style to grab attention with the meaning: It is a matter of fact or there is no doubt about it.
